fetch_status: Top-3 WebSearch results covered Nested Learning (Google Nov 2025), Recursive Language Models (Prime Intellect/MIT Oct 2025), World Models vs LMs paradigm shift. WebFetch all 403, WebSearch fallback yielded full technical mechanisms.

atoms.post_title: Nested Learning: New ML paradigm for continual learning
atoms.text: Nested Learning treats AI not as a single program but as a system of nested learning processes that update at DIFFERENT RATES; architecture and training rules are unified as different levels of optimization.
Hope is a self-modifying architecture proof-of-concept for Nested Learning; memory becomes a spectrum of modules updated at different frequencies, dynamically allocating knowledge across time scales.
RLMs let an LLM use a persistent Python REPL to inspect/transform input and spawn sub-LLM copies; main model context stays short and clean while context-folding happens via code and recursion.
RLMs reach effective context length about 2 orders of magnitude longer than base; 10M+ tokens on certain workloads, by trading context-window memory for code+recursion control.

atoms.post_title: World Models vs Language Models paradigm shift
atoms.text: World models predict latent representations of environment states; LMs predict next tokens in linguistic space. The shift moves from statistical text patterns to causal dynamics of physical reality. JEPA exemplifies the direction.

Next-latent-prediction transformers converge to BELIEF STATES — compressed history representations sufficient for prediction — formally bridging transformer LM and world-model architectures via belief-state theory.

atoms.post_title: HCLSM Hierarchical Causal Latent State Machines (2603.29090)
atoms.text: Hierarchical Causal Latent State Machines separate object representations and maintain multi-scale temporal dynamics; flat latent world models lose causal structure by collapsing temporal scales.

atoms.post_title: The Mouth is Not the Brain (2601.17094)
atoms.text: Energy-Based World Models bridge to language generation; the LM is the 'mouth' (rendering) and the world model is the 'brain' (causal state); separation of mouth from brain is the architectural principle.

atoms.post_title: Prior-Data Fitted Networks (PFN) for causal inference
atoms.text: PFNs do Bayesian inference purely through in-context learning; flexible efficient predictions without additional training per task. In-context learning becomes a universal inference engine.
In Prime Intellect's RLMEnv: the main RLM gets ONLY a Python REPL; subordinate LLMs get heavy tools (web search, file access). Capability segregation by tool budget is the safety + efficiency axis.

atoms.post_title: Linear Spatial World Models in LLMs (arxiv 2506.02996)
atoms.text: Linear spatial world models emerge inside LLMs without explicit training; LMs spontaneously develop world-model-like substructure when scaled, contrary to the LeCun 'doomed' framing.

Architecture and training rules are fundamentally the SAME thing — different levels of optimization, each with its own context flow and update rate. The duality between design-time and run-time vanishes.
